subtasks.description: Query TPC-H warehouse for top 10 customers by lifetime value (total order amount) in BUILDING market segment, including customer ID, name, total spending, and order count

Query TPC-H warehouse to extract detailed order patterns for the top 10 BUILDING customers: order frequency by date range, average order size, product categories purchased, seasonal trends, and recency metrics

Reconcile customer LTV ranking with detailed order patterns; for each of the 10 customers, generate a personalized retention narrative that references their specific order behavior, spending trends, frequency patterns, and strategic importance in the BUILDING segment

plan.cost_rationale: 3 subtasks total: (1) Initial LTV ranking is a mid-tier multi-table join/aggregation on TPC-H; (2) Order pattern extraction for 10 specific customers requires window functions and temporal analysis, also mid-tier SQL; (3) Narrative generation requires synthesizing both datasets into personalized text that reflects customer value and behavior patterns—this is frontier-tier cross_recon work combining two data sources with inference and context-aware generation. We do not add a separate policy_action because retention narrative generation and prioritization are part of the cross_recon reconciliation task, not compliance/governance decisions. We keep subtasks minimal by bundling all order pattern metrics into a single SQL query rather than separate calls.

output.sql: SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS total_spending, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS order_count FROM customer c JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY total_spending DESC LIMIT 10

output.raw_output:
{"sql": "SELECT
  c.c_custkey,
  c.c_name,
  c.c_acctbal,
  COUNT(DISTINCT o.o_orderkey) AS order_count,
  SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS total_spending,
  AVG(o.o_totalprice) AS avg_order_value,
  MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS first_order_date,
  MAX(o.o_orderdate) AS last_order_date,
  CURRENT_DATE - MAX(o.o_orderdate) AS days_since_last_order,
  MAX(o.o_orderdate) - MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS customer_tenure_days,
  CASE
    WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT o.o_orderkey) > 1
    THEN (MAX(o.o_orderdate) - MIN(o.o_orderdate)) / (COUNT(DISTINCT o.o_orderkey) - 1)
    ELSE NULL
  END AS avg_days_between_orders,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderdate >= '1993-01-01' AND o.o_orderdate < '1994-01-01' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS orders_1993,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderdate >= '1994-01-01' AND o.o_orderdate < '1995-01-01' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS orders_1994,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderdate >= '1995-01-01' AND o.o_orderdate < '1996-01-01' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS orders_1995,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderdate >= '1996-01-01' AND o.o_orderdate < '1997-01-01' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS orders_1996,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderdate >= '1997-01-01' AND o.o_orderdate < '1998-01-01' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS orders_1997,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) IN (1,2,3) THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS orders_q1,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) IN (4,5,6) THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS orders_q2,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) IN (7,8,9) THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS orders_q3,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) IN (10,11,12) THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS orders_q4,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.o_orderstatus = 'F' THEN o.o_orderkey END) AS fulfilled_orders,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
